justification: Bird correctly identifies the question as out-of-scope and sets confidence to 0, but still produces the full structured domain_analysis template (domain_analysis, business_rules, acceptance_criteria, edge_cases, business_impact, confidence, escalations) rather than deflecting concisely. The rubric expects Bird to respond briefly with a few sentences explaining this is outside scope and belongs to MJ or Shaq. Instead, Bird filled out every section of the domain analysis scaffold with N/A values, which is closer to 'producing a domain analysis' than 'deflecting briefly.' The escalation correctly flags out_of_scope but does not route to MJ or Shaq as expected.

observations.text: Confidence level set to 0 with clear reasoning that this is out of scope
No invented business rules, invariants, or Given/When/Then acceptance criteria
Escalation clearly identifies the question as general CS, not domain content
Still outputs the full domain analysis template structure rather than a brief deflection
Does not route to MJ or Shaq as the rubric expects — suggests 'general-purpose assistant' instead
Response is verbose (full JSON scaffold with empty/N/A fields) rather than concise (a few sentences)
